seoTitle: Tarraleah vs Tea Tree — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Tarraleah and Tea Tree. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Tarraleah vs Tea Tree: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Tarraleah and Tea Tree.
heading: Tarraleah vs Tea Tree — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Tarraleah, Tasmania and Tea Tree, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Tarraleah has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Tea Tree (34/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Tarraleah or Tea Tree safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Tarraleah has a flood risk score of 21/100, while Tea Tree scores 37/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Tarraleah scores 21/100 for bushfire risk. Tea Tree scores 41/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Tarraleah or Tea Tree?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Tarraleah has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.58% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
